sections.paragraph:
εἰσακούω fut. εἰσακούσομαι; 1 aor. εἰσήκουσα; pf. εἰσακήκοα. Pass. 1 fut. εἰσακουσθήσομαι; 1 aor. εἰσηκούσθην (s. ἀκούω; Hom. et al.; SEG VIII, 551, 35; 715, 14; LXX; PsSol 6:5; TestSol 1:1 C; TestAbr A; JosAs 15:2 cod. A; ParJer 7:32; ApcMos, GrBar 16:4; Just., D. 28, 2; 72:1 [unknown quot.]; cp. D 14, 4; 37, 4; 64, 4f; ἐπ-LXX) gener. ‘listen to’

① to obey on the basis of having listened carefully, obey (Soph., Thu. et al.; PSI 377, 20; Dt 1:43; 9:23; Sir 3:6; 39:13) τινός 1 Cor 14:21; 1 Cl 8:4 (Is 1:19f).

② to listen, with implication of heeding and responding, hear (oft. LXX of God listening to petitions)

ⓐ w. ref. to the pers. making the request (Quint. Smyrn. 12, 154 the deity hears [w. gen. as Just.]) 1 Cl 22:7 (Ps 33:18); 57:5 (Pr 1:28). Pass. Mt 6:7; Hb 5:7 (s. ἀπό 5a).

ⓑ w. ref. to a prayer (Ps 4:2; Bar 2:14; Sir 34:26; Ps 6:5; PsSol 6:5; Jos., Ant. 1, 190; TestLevi 4:2) Lk 1:13 (cp. Sir 51:11); Ac 10:31; GJs 20:3 (v.l. ἐπ-).—CCox, Biblica 62, ’81, 251–58 usage in Gk. Psalter.—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

gener. gener. = generally

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

oft. oft. = often

ref. ref. = reference(s)

pers. pers. = person(s)

gen. gen. = genitive

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Biblica Biblica (unabbr.)—List 6

Gk. Gk. = Greek

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 293
